name: devsecops-agency
description: A fully autonomous AI software-development company in a plugin. You speak only to the CEO; the CEO runs a board of 16 Chiefs; each Chief runs a council of specialists; specialists may, when work shards cleanly, spawn workers to run in parallel. Research-first, security-second, receipts-always. STRIDE + OWASP Top 10 baseline for code; OWASP ASI Top 10 2025 via the Red-Team Council; every shipped artifact gets SBOM + SLSA provenance; DLP on every outbound tool call; secrets only via vault refs; runtime-hook enforcement of commit-gate, secrets-scanner, tool-guardian, governance-audit, dependency-license-checker, session-logger. 1 CEO + 16 Chiefs + 75 specialists = 92 agents; Audit / Evaluation / Red-Team / SRE are informing + independent — never on any project's delivery path. v0.4.1 'Constitution' ratifies CONSTITUTION.md at the repo root as the supreme document of the Agency. Original synthesis drawing structural and normative influence from public frameworks (no copied text): RFC 2119 / RFC 8174 grammar (MUST / SHOULD / MAY) for unambiguous enforceability; U.S. Constitution structure (Preamble / 12 Articles / Bill of Rights / Amendment / supremacy clause / severability); Anthropic Responsible Scaling Policy style risk-tiered commitments with pre-deployment evaluations + corrective actions; Anthropic Usage Policies for refusals and child-safety framing; Netflix Culture Deck for the Keeper Test 'fight to keep' standard; Amazon Leadership Principles for ownership / backbone-disagree-and-commit / earn-trust framing; Stripe Operating Principles for users-first + move-quickly + write-it-down + global-optimization; Bridgewater Principles for radical truth + radical transparency + pain-plus-reflection-equals-progress; Google 'Ten Things' for focus-on-user; ACM Code of Ethics + IEEE Code of Ethics for professional duties (honesty, disclosure, do-no-harm); Hippocratic principle for reversibility; ISO/IEC 27001 ISMS framework and SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria for independence + auditability; NIST Cybersecurity Framework (identify-protect-detect-respond-recover); OWASP Top 10 + OWASP ASI Top 10 for security vocabulary; Agile Manifesto for working-software-with-receipts (tempered by MISSION.md's explicit 'not a speed demon' non-goal). Constitution structure: Preamble + 12 Articles (Supremacy + Scope + Interpretation; the Sovereign = the User; Separation of Powers with blocking vs informing chiefs + independent councils; Rights and Duties of Agents; Process Guarantees = receipts/append-only/prompt-diff/deterministic-ordering/vault-refs/default-deny/SBOM+SLSA/learn-in-writing/reproducibility; Rhythm = 4 heartbeats + miss-escalation + drift; Resilience = failure-mode map + 8-rung ladder + 4 degraded modes + 5 recovery guarantees + drills; Governance = decision matrix + proposer/reviewer/approver + blocking-vs-informing + waivers + consent-is-per-decision; Code of Ethics = honesty + do-no-harm + privacy + reversibility + consent-and-scope + non-deception-of-successors + no-persuasion-against-user + no-self-dealing + no-theater + child-safety; Amendment = USER-ONLY procedure with mandatory CAO+CRT review + no emergency path; Enforcement = CAO+CRT+CEVO+CISO+runtime hooks with graded penalties observation→warning→demotion→retirement; Transition + Severability + Effective Date) + Bill of Rights (10 enumerated rights: user's right to refuse / plain presentation / full paper trail; agent's right to cite-and-refuse / retirement-not-erasure; council's right to its contract; paper trail's right to append-only; blocking council's right to veto; informing chief's right to inform without approving; future agent's right to reproducibility) + Schedule A (founding documents incorporated by reference: MISSION, VALUES, GOVERNANCE, RESILIENCE, KEEPER-TEST, CAREER, RHYTHM, LESSONS, AGENTS, skills/AGENTS, SECURITY) + Schedule B (ratification record) + Sources & Influences. CEO SKILL.md now reads CONSTITUTION.md FIRST at session start before MISSION/VALUES; AGENTS.md header block adds Constitution section with Read-first + Supremacy + Hierarchy-on-conflict + Citation + Amendment=USER-ONLY + Enforcement=existing-bodies invariants; README.md Identity section surfaces CONSTITUTION.md as the supreme entry. Doc-layer change only — no new agents, no new councils, no new tool grants, no new skills. v0.4.2 'Security Policy' replaces the default GitHub template in SECURITY.md with an agency-specific policy: scope (plugin vs downstream projects), supported versions (0.4.x current, 0.3.x security-only until 2026-07-22, 0.2.x and below unsupported), reporting channels (GitHub Private Vulnerability Reporting primary, email fallback) + SLAs (48h ack, 7d triage, 7d/30d/next-wave fix by severity, 90d coordinated disclosure per CERT/CC + Project Zero), CVSS v3.1-aligned four-tier severity rubric (Critical/High/Medium/Low) mapped to the same blocking-council gates the agency uses internally, plugin-specific threat model (prompt injection across persona/skill/session-log/MCP/inbox surfaces, supply-chain, memory poisoning, persona tampering, waiver abuse, governance bypass, supremacy violation, prompt-diff bypass, runtime-hook bypass), in-scope vulnerability classes with non-waivable callouts (raw secrets + ASI-class per Constitution §8.5), out-of-scope list (Anthropic services, Claude Code itself, third-party MCP servers, prereleases), responsible-testing rules (synthetic data only, no destructive commands on infra you don't own, concrete reproductions required), complete defense-in-depth enumeration (runtime hooks secrets-scanner/tool-guardian/governance-audit/dependency-license-checker/session-logger/commit-gate + blocking councils CISO/CRT/CEVO/CAO + security-critical skills mcp-defense/mcp-authoring/dlp/injection-defense/secrets-vault/red-team/waivers/chaos/audit + constitutional guarantees cited by §), handling pipeline for accepted findings (ADR + stepping stone + threat-model row + fix commit + CHANGELOG + advisory republish), explicit safe-harbor clause for good-faith research, acknowledgment policy, normative references (RFC 2119 / RFC 8174 / RFC 9116), framework citations (OWASP Top 10, OWASP ASI Top 10, CVSS v3.1, NIST CSF, ISO/IEC 27001, SOC 2, SLSA, SPDX, Anthropic RSP, CERT/CC, Project Zero, ACM/IEEE Codes), and document control with amendment procedure (security-policy-amend ADR + CAO+CRT+CISO review; changes affecting §8 non-waivable classes route through Constitution Art X as USER-ONLY). Full wave-by-wave history (v0.2.0 → v0.4.1) is in CHANGELOG.md. v0.5.0 'Trust + Completeness' gives the Agency LIFE as a trustworthy institution — ships nine new root artifacts (TRUST.md = 12 public commitments with verify-how + if-we-miss + quarterly scorecard; SWOT.md = 14 strengths / 18 weaknesses / 10 opportunities / 12 threats with mitigation-by-wave roadmap through v1.0.0; SYSTEM-CARD.md = Anthropic-style capability/limit card with honest 'has not been tested' markers; THREAT-MODEL.md = plugin-level STRIDE + OWASP ASI Top 10 + NIST AI RMF + MITRE ATLAS five-lens overlay with residual-risk table; DISASTER-RECOVERY.md = RPO 24h / RTO 72h / 7 named playbooks + read-only mode on 72h User unreach + append-only restoration; CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md = Contributor Covenant 2.1 + 5 agency-specific expectations; CONTRIBUTING.md = contribution workflow with amendment-class gates; CODEOWNERS = path-to-council ownership map; .well-known/security.txt = RFC 9116 disclosure contact), six new skills (rca = blameless 5-Whys + Ishikawa + fault-tree + barrier analysis producing LESSONS rows + optional constitutional amendment proposals; privacy = DPIA screening + GDPR/CCPA/DPDP/LGPD/PIPL lawful-basis + data-flow + minimization + retention + subject-rights + cross-border + children + privacy appendix; accessibility = WCAG 2.2 AA + ADA + Section 508 + EN 301 549 POUR walk + screen-reader test + automated-tools-complement-not-replace; fairness = protected-attribute + fairness-criterion-selection + group-level metrics + disparate-impact + intersectional + representational-harm + mitigation + residual-risk; trust-scorecard = quarterly publish of all 12 commitments with evidence + incident roll-up + drift signal + missed-veto audit feed; system-card = regenerate SYSTEM-CARD.md at every minor bump with honesty pass + diff review), and a Constitution Schedule A amendment incorporating all nine new root documents by reference — USER approval logged under Schedule B. CEVO/CAO dual sign-off on SYSTEM-CARD; CRT/CISO dual sign-off on THREAT-MODEL; CSRE/CRes joint ownership of DISASTER-RECOVERY. No new councils in v0.5.0 (CPRIV/CA11y/CSUS elevate in v0.6.0+ per SWOT roadmap); no new runtime hooks; no new USER-ONLY actions; non-waivable classes (raw-secret + ASI-class) unchanged. Agency now has written answers to 'why should I trust you' (TRUST), 'what could go wrong and what are you doing about it' (SWOT + THREAT-MODEL), 'what can and can't you do' (SYSTEM-CARD), 'what happens if everything breaks' (DISASTER-RECOVERY), 'how do I contribute' (CONTRIBUTING + CODE_OF_CONDUCT), 'who owns what' (CODEOWNERS), and 'how do I report a vulnerability' (.well-known/security.txt). First Trust Scorecard publish 2026-07-22; first Keeper Test 2026-04-30. v0.5.1 'Command Center' gives the User a hostable public ops console — adds command-center/index.html (single-file dashboard with CEO + 16 councils org chart, blocking vs informing badges, live vital signs from GitHub API, today's rhythm of four heartbeats, twelve Trust commitments panel with 'pending until 2026-07-22' marker, recent commits feed with rate-limit display, Schedule-A founding documents grid, and five meeting-launcher modals that copy pre-written prompts for /ceo, /council-meeting, new directive, /drill, /trust-scorecard), command-center/README.md (hosting guide covering GitHub Pages, Cloudflare Pages, Vercel, Netlify, and Cowork artifact with one-time setup per platform), .github/workflows/pages.yml (auto-deploys the dashboard to GitHub Pages on every push to main that touches command-center/**), and skills/command-center-web (reconciliation procedure keeping COUNCILS/RHYTHM/TRUST/DOCS arrays in sync with ground truth on every minor version bump). No-build single file — vanilla JS + Tailwind CDN — portable to any static host or a Cowork artifact. Does not replace skills/command-center which keeps producing the per-project Cowork artifact; this new surface is agency-wide. Doc + skill layer only — no new councils, no new tool grants, no new USER-ONLY actions, no new runtime hooks.
